rows.notes: Pounds value visually confirmed as 550 (from gpt-5-mini), overriding gemini-flash's 530. Description based on gemini-flash for 'Wittenham' and 'Mich.r', which visually appear more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Wittersham' and 'Michl.'.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash as it correctly combines '1803' (from the margin) with the description. gpt-5-mini incorrectly split '1803' into a separate header row (R7) and added 'Mar. 26' to the description of this line (R8), which belongs to the subsequent entry.

rows.description: 26 Corn Rent do
rows.notes: Visually confirmed as 8 pounds, 10 shillings, 0 pence. The '8' is clearly aligned under the pounds column and '10' under the shillings column. gpt-5-mini incorrectly interpreted this as 0 pounds, 8 shillings, 10 pence. Description taken from gemini-flash for including '26'.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Corn rent (item 26) received for Merton, amounting to £8 10s 0d.

rows.description: - 30 by Mr. Glubb, Bursar, Increment Mich.l 1802
rows.notes: Consensus on whole monetary amounts. Description and fraction taken from gemini-flash: 'Mich.l 1802' is more accurate than gpt-5-mini's 'Mids.', and the fractional mark visually appears to be ¾ (0.75) rather than ½. The leading dash and 'by' are also more accurately transcribed by gemini-flash.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Increment (augmentation) paid by the Bursar Mr Glubb for Michaelmas 1802, amounting to £163 16s 8.75d.
